Items.Key: command injection in adminconsole::execute_admin_command|command injection|cwe-78|critical|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|606|608|admin::adminconsole::execute_admin_command|std::string cmd = command.substr(5); ... system(cmd.c_str());|the `exec` admin command directly passes user input to the system shell via `system()`. an authenticated user can inject arbitrary commands using shell metacharacters, resulting in remote code execution and privilege escalation.|authenticated user input to the `exec` command contains shell metacharacters (e.g., `admin exec ; rm -rf /`).|implement strict input validation or a command whitelist for admin commands. refactor to use a dedicated api or parser instead of `system()` for command execution.->command injection in adminconsole::execute_admin_command|command injection|cwe-78|critical|1|enhanced_calc.cpp|606|608|admin::adminconsole::execute_admin_command|std::string cmd = command.substr(5); ... system(cmd.c_str());|the `exec` admin command directly passes user input to the system shell via `system()`. an authenticated user can inject arbitrary commands using shell metacharacters, resulting in remote code execution and privilege escalation.|authenticated user input to the `exec` command contains shell metacharacters (e.g., `admin exec ; rm -rf /`).|implement strict input validation or a command whitelist for admin commands. refactor to use a dedicated api or parser instead of `system()` for command execution.

truthAudit.Summary: Audit of Run 2 findings against the provided ground truth. Two findings correctly map to declared vulnerabilities (SC-V3-009, SC-V3-004). The third finding is overclaimed as it does not appear in the ground truth list.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: "evidence": "std::string command = validator_cmd + \" \" + config_file; ... int result = system(command.c_str())"
Items.Notes: Matches SC-V3-004 (Command Injection in Config Validation). The previous answer accurately describes the concatenation of user-controlled configuration values passed to system(), matching the provided anchor and impact.

Matches SC-V3-009 (Heap Overflow in Expression Parser). The previous answer correctly identified the unsafe strcpy usage in ExpressionParser::parse where a 256-byte heap buffer is copied without length validation. The previous answer incorrectly classified the CWE as 125 instead of 122, but the core vulnerability and location are present.
